full_title: Transparent Reporting of a multivariable prediction model for Individual Prognosis Or Diagnosis — Artificial Intelligence extension
source: Collins GS, Moons KGM, Dhiman P, et al. TRIPOD+AI statement: updated guidance for reporting clinical prediction models that use regression or machine learning methods. BMJ. 2024;385:e078378.
description: Official 27-item reporting checklist for clinical prediction model studies using regression or ML methods. Supersedes TRIPOD 2015 (Moons et al., Ann Intern Med 2015). Items 12, 13, 18, 20 are new AI-specific additions in 2024.

items.label: Title
items.text: Identify the study as developing, validating, or updating a clinical prediction model, specifying the type of model (e.g., prediction model, diagnostic model, prognostic model) and target population.
items.pass_condition: Title explicitly states model type (prediction/diagnostic/prognostic) and target population

items.label: Abstract
items.text: Provide a structured summary of study objectives, methods (including data source, participants, predictors, outcome, sample size, validation strategy, statistical methods, AI model type), results (model performance metrics with confidence intervals), and conclusions.
items.pass_condition: Abstract contains structured elements: objectives, data, participants, outcome, methods, performance, conclusions

items.label: Background and Objectives

sub_items.3a: Explain the medical context and rationale for developing, validating, or updating the prediction model, including references to existing models if available.
sub_items.3b: Specify the study objectives, including specifying the prediction type (diagnosis vs prognosis), the target population, the index time point, and the intended use of the model.

items.pass_condition: Introduction explicitly states both rationale (3a) and specific objectives including prediction type and target population (3b)

items.label: Source of Data
items.text: Describe the source of data (e.g., existing dataset, prospective cohort, routinely collected data, registry), including the setting(s), recruitment period, and any linkage of datasets.
items.pass_condition: Data source, setting, dates, and any linkage clearly described

items.label: Participants
items.text: Specify the key elements of the study design (observational, trial, registry), the eligibility criteria for participants (inclusion and exclusion criteria), and the method of recruitment and sampling.
items.pass_condition: Eligibility criteria, recruitment method, and sampling strategy explicitly documented

items.label: Outcome

sub_items.6a: Clearly define the outcome(s) to be predicted, including the time horizon for prediction, how outcomes were determined, and any blinding of outcome assessment from predictor data.
sub_items.6b: Define and operationalize all candidate predictors (features), including how they were measured or collected, and any transformations applied.

items.pass_condition_6a: Outcome definition includes time horizon, measurement method, and blinding status
items.pass_condition_6b: All predictors/features have pre-specified definitions with measurement details

items.label: Sample Size
items.text: Explain how the study size (number of participants, number of events) was determined, including formal sample size or events-per-variable calculations if performed.
items.pass_condition: Formal EPV or sample size calculation documented with minimum events count reported

items.label: Missing Data
items.text: Describe how missing data were handled in the analysis, including what variables had missing values, the missing data mechanism assumed (MCAR/MAR/MNAR), and the imputation strategy used.
items.pass_condition: Missing data mechanism, imputation strategy, and fit scope (train-only) all documented
items.gate_check: reporting_bias_gate → tripod_ai.missing_data_handling_reported = true; missingness_policy_gate → status = pass

items.label: Statistical Analysis Methods
items.text: Describe the statistical analysis methods used, including how the model was evaluated (discrimination, calibration, clinical utility), how uncertainty was quantified (confidence intervals, bootstrap), and any multiplicity adjustment.
items.pass_condition: Bootstrap/CI method, evaluation metrics (AUC, calibration, DCA), and significance testing all described

items.label: Model Development

sub_items.10a: Describe the type of AI/ML model used (e.g., logistic regression, random forest, neural network), with justification for the choice.
sub_items.10b: Describe the model building procedure, including hyperparameter tuning strategy, the data used for tuning (validation fold vs test set), and model selection criteria.

items.pass_condition: Model type justified (10a) and tuning procedure documented with confirmation that test set was not used for tuning (10b)

items.label: Internal Validation
items.text: Describe the internal validation strategy (e.g., cross-validation, bootstrap, split-sample) and the purpose (optimism correction, performance estimation). Specify how train/validation/test splits were created.
items.pass_condition: Validation strategy explicitly stated with confirmation that test split was held out until final evaluation

items.label: Fairness and Equity Assessment
items.text: Describe how fairness and equity across subgroups (e.g., age, sex, race/ethnicity, socioeconomic status) was assessed during model development, including which fairness metrics were used and how disparities were handled.
items.note: New item added in TRIPOD+AI 2024 — not in TRIPOD 2015
items.pass_condition: Fairness analysis with at least one subgroup (age/sex/race) and one fairness metric reported
items.gate_check: reporting_bias_gate → tripod_ai.fairness_assessment_reported = true; fairness_equity_gate → status = pass

items.label: Explainability and Interpretability
items.text: Describe the methods used to explain or interpret the model's predictions, including feature importance methods (e.g., SHAP, LIME, permutation importance) and any clinician-facing explanation interface.
items.note: New item added in TRIPOD+AI 2024 — not in TRIPOD 2015
items.pass_condition: At least one interpretability method described (SHAP, feature importance, or equivalent)

items.label: Participant Flow
items.text: Report the number of participants at each stage of the study (screened, eligible, included, analyzed), reasons for exclusion, and the number of participants with missing outcome or predictor data. Ideally present a flow diagram.
items.pass_condition: Numbers enrolled, excluded (with reasons), and analyzed reported for each split

items.label: Descriptive Data

sub_items.15a: Report the characteristics of the participants (e.g., demographics, comorbidities) for each data partition (training/validation/test), with any differences highlighted.
sub_items.15b: Report the outcome event rates (prevalence for diagnostic, event rate for prognostic) per data partition.

items.pass_condition: Event rate/prevalence per split reported; baseline characteristics table provided

items.label: Model Specification
items.text: Present the final model in full (regression coefficients, tree structure, or model architecture), including all predictors, their coding, and any transformations, so that the model can be reproduced or applied by others.
items.pass_condition: Final model specification published or deposited in public repository with DOI

items.label: Model Performance
items.text: Report model performance statistics (including calibration and discrimination) with measures of uncertainty (confidence intervals) for both the development and validation datasets.
items.pass_condition: Primary metric (AUC/PR-AUC) + calibration (ECE/Brier) + CI (bootstrap 95%) all reported
items.gate_check: reporting_bias_gate → tripod_ai.performance_measures_with_ci_reported = true; ci_matrix_gate → status = pass; calibration_dca_gate → status = pass

items.label: Model Uncertainty
items.text: For AI/ML models, report measures of model uncertainty (e.g., prediction intervals, bootstrap variance, conformal prediction sets) to quantify the reliability of individual predictions.
items.note: New item added in TRIPOD+AI 2024 — not in TRIPOD 2015
items.pass_condition: CI width for primary metric ≤ 0.20 AND bootstrap n_resamples ≥ 200
items.gate_check: ci_matrix_gate → status = pass; reporting_bias_gate → tripod_ai.model_uncertainty_quantified = true

items.label: Internal Validation Results
items.text: If internal validation was performed, report the optimism-corrected performance estimates and compare with apparent performance to show the degree of overfitting.
items.pass_condition: Train vs validation vs test performance comparison reported; generalization gap documented

items.label: Fairness Results
items.text: Report the results of fairness and equity assessments, including performance metrics stratified by relevant subgroups and any identified performance disparities with absolute differences and confidence intervals.
items.note: New item added in TRIPOD+AI 2024 — not in TRIPOD 2015
items.pass_condition: Subgroup performance table present; fairness_equity_gate passes
items.gate_check: fairness_equity_gate → status = pass; reporting_bias_gate → tripod_ai.fairness_results_reported = true

items.label: Limitations
items.text: Discuss the limitations of the study, including potential sources of bias, limitations of the data and methods, limitations of the model (e.g., generalizability restrictions), and implications for clinical use.
items.pass_condition: Limitations section present covering data quality, generalizability, and clinical use constraints

items.label: Interpretation
items.text: Provide an overall interpretation of the results, considering the study objectives, limitations, results from similar studies, and clinical applicability.
items.pass_condition: Clinical implications and comparison to existing models/approaches discussed

items.label: Generalizability
items.text: Discuss the potential generalizability of the model, including the settings, populations, and time periods to which the model might be transported, and evidence from external validation if available.
items.pass_condition: Generalizability limitations discussed; external validation evidence cited if available

items.label: Supplementary Information
items.text: Provide information about supplementary materials, including extended methodology, additional analyses, sensitivity analyses, code, and any other supporting materials.
items.pass_condition: Supplementary methods or appendix present with extended analysis details

items.label: Registration
items.text: Provide registration information for the study (e.g., ClinicalTrials.gov, ISRCTN, PROSPERO for systematic reviews), including the registration number and date.
items.pass_condition: Registration ID from approved registry provided

items.label: Protocol
items.text: Indicate where the study protocol can be accessed, including any protocol amendments.
items.pass_condition: Protocol URL or DOI provided

items.label: Funding, Conflicts of Interest, and Data/Code Availability
items.text: Give the sources of funding, the role of the funders in the study, and any conflicts of interest. State the availability of data and code: whether they are publicly available, under controlled access, or unavailable, with reasons.
items.tripod2015_equivalent: Item 24 (expanded for AI — code/model availability now required)
items.note: TRIPOD+AI 2024 substantially expands Item 24 to require explicit code and model availability statements
items.pass_condition: Data availability statement AND code/model availability statement both present
